heading: Repealed. , title I, § 1,Aug. 27, 1935, ch. 74049 Stat. 872
label: 1 to 5 Repealed. , title I, § 1,Aug. 27, 1935, ch. 74049 Stat. 872
notes: Section 1, , , set forth the short title of act as “National Prohibition Act”.act Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 85, § 141 Stat. 305

Section 2, , , provided that this title apply to the United States and all territory subject to its jurisdiction, including the Territory of Hawaii and the Virgin Islands.act Nov. 23, 1921, ch. 134, § 342 Stat. 223

Section 3, , , authorized all laws relating to the manufacture and taxation of and traffic in intoxicating liquor, and all penalties for violations of such laws that were in force on , to continue in force, as to both beverage and nonbeverage liquor, except to the extent provisions of such laws were directly in conflict with provisions of this title.act Nov. 23, 1921, ch. 134, § 542 Stat. 223Oct. 28, 1919

Section 4, acts , title II, § 1, ; , , defined “liquor” or “intoxicating liquor”, “person”, “commissioner”, “application”, “permit”, “bond”, and “regulation”.Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 8541 Stat. 307Nov. 23, 1921, ch. 134, § 142 Stat. 222

Section 5, , , provided that any act authorized to be done by the commissioner could be performed by any assistant or agent designated by him for that purpose, and any records required to be filed with the commissioner could be filed with an assistant commissioner or any other person designated to receive such records.act Oct. 28, 1919, ch. 85, title II, § 141 Stat. 308
